content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://www.massgeneralbrigham.org/en/about/newsroom/articles/pros-and-cons-of-intermittent-fasting", "source_title": "Intermittent Fasting Benefits, Risks, and How it Works", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "But is it a smart strategy, or a passing fad? \u201cIt\u2019s not a magic cure for losing weight, but the research on intermittent fasting is promisin...", "direct_quote": "But is it a smart strategy, or a passing fad? \u201cIt\u2019s not a magic cure for losing weight, but the research on intermittent fasting is promising,\u201d says Mary Hyer, RD, LDN, CCRP.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "That depends. \u201cThere are lots of options to choose from,\u201d Hyer says. \u201cBut in the scientific world, you have to go at least 12 hours for it t...", "direct_quote": "That depends. \u201cThere are lots of options to choose from,\u201d Hyer says. \u201cBut in the scientific world, you have to go at least 12 hours for it to be considered a fast.\u201d How does intermittent fasting work?", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Your body usually burns glucose (sugar) for energy, but after about 10 to 12 hours without food your glucose stores run low.", "direct_quote": "Your body usually burns glucose (sugar) for energy, but after about 10 to 12 hours without food your glucose stores run low.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
